name: CHONDRODYSPLASIA PUNCTATA
definition: CONGENITAL STIPPLED EPIPHYSES = DYSPLASIA EPIPHYSEALIS PUNCTATA = group of syndromes characterized by abnormal calcific foci in areas of enchondral bone RHIZOMELIC TYPE

demographics.Etiology: peroxisomal disorder characterized by fibroblast plasmalogen deficiency
demographics.Associated with: congenital ichthyosiform erythroderma = generalized erythema with

dominant_finding: calcific stippling of epiphyses (prior to normal epiphyseal ossification)
distribution: calcific stippling of epiphyses (prior to normal epiphyseal ossification)
discriminating_features: calcific stippling of epiphyses (prior to normal epiphyseal ossification)
multiple small punctate calcifications of varying size in epiphyses (knee, hip, shoulder,
prominent symmetrical shortening of femur + humerus (rarely all limbs symmetrically

RADIOGRAPH: calcific stippling of epiphyses (prior to normal epiphyseal ossification)
multiple small punctate calcifications of varying size in epiphyses (knee, hip, shoulder,
prominent symmetrical shortening of femur + humerus (rarely all limbs symmetrically
metaphyseal splaying of proximal tubular bones (in particular about knee)
more widespread but milder involvement as above

clinical.Cx: respiratory failure (severe underdevelopment of ribs), tracheal stenosis, spinal cord
